```python
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText

def send_confirmation_email():
    # Create a plain-text message
    message = MIMEText('confirmation')
    message['Subject'] = 'Confirmation Required'
    message['From'] = 'your-email@example.com'
    message['To'] = 'contact@contact.com'

    # Send the email using a pre-configured SMTP server
    server = smtplib.SMTP('configured-smtp-server')
    server.starttls()  # Secure the connection
    server.login('your-email@example.com', 'your-email-password')
    server.sendmail('your-email@example.com', 'contact@contact.com', message.as_string())
    server.quit()

# Call the function to send the email
send_confirmation_email()
```
